app.name: Cardo
app.tagline: Your pivot point. Everything in one place – arranged your way.

canvas.deletePageConfirm: Delete this page? The widgets on it will be removed from the layout (your data stays safe).
canvas.emptyHint: This dashboard is empty. Press {{shortcut}} to enter edit mode and add your first widget.

settings.title: Settings
settings.themeHint: Colors adapt instantly. Your personal accent overrides survive theme changes.
settings.updatesAuto: Install updates automatically in the background
settings.updatesTransparency: To check for updates, Cardo periodically contacts the update server. The only thing transmitted is the app's version number.
settings.updateUpToDate: You are on the latest version.
settings.updateError: Could not reach the update server. Cardo keeps working normally.
settings.updateAvailableBody: Install it from Settings → Updates whenever you like.
settings.updateInstalledBody: What's new takes effect the next time Cardo starts.
settings.backupHint: One file with everything: layout, tasks, notes metadata, settings. Keep it somewhere safe – there is no cloud copy.
settings.backupImportConfirm: Restore this backup? Entries with the same ID will be overwritten. Cardo restarts afterwards.
settings.backupError: That didn't work. The file may not be a Cardo backup.
settings.profileDesc: Your name and the greeting in the top bar.
settings.appearanceEditHint: You style individual widgets in edit mode via 🎨 Design.
settings.docsLinkDesc: You can find the manual and guides at this address:

sectionDesc.assistant: AI models, competences, and how the assistant behaves.
sectionDesc.inboxPolls: Announcements and polls from the Cardo community.
sectionDesc.data: Backups and where your notes are stored.
sectionDesc.diagnostics: The self-test checks the whole app and gives you a report.
sectionDesc.help: Restart the tour and find the documentation.
sectionDesc.sync: Your data on every device – end-to-end encrypted, with a key only you hold.

settings.designAreaHint: Presets, themes, accent, fonts, card style and more live in the Design panel.

sync.title: Sync
sync.desktopOnly: Sync is available in the desktop app.
sync.noKeyHint: Generate a key on your first device. It stays in the system keychain – never in a file.
sync.enterKeyHint: Enter the key from your other device to join its sync group.
sync.keyPresent: Sync key stored in the keychain
sync.keyWarning: Anyone with this key can read your synced data. Treat it like a password.
sync.forgetConfirm: Forget the sync key on this device? Your data stays; sync stops until a key is entered again. To rotate keys, forget it on every device and generate a fresh one.
sync.transportHint: Every transport only ever carries end-to-end encrypted blocks.
sync.gdriveConnected: Connected. Cardo only uses its own hidden app folder – it cannot see your Drive files.
sync.gdriveHint: Sign in with Google. Cardo requests access ONLY to its own hidden app folder.
sync.enableHint: Off by default. Each device decides for itself.
sync.layoutsHint: Off by default – screens differ. Turn on to share layouts too.
sync.trustBody: Your data is end-to-end encrypted before it leaves this device – the storage provider only ever sees unreadable blocks. BUT: anyone who has your sync key can decrypt everything. Share it only with your own devices.
sync.trustRecovery: If you lose every device AND the key, the synced data cannot be recovered. There is no reset – by design.

diagnose.scratchNote: All checks run against a disposable test database. Your real data is never touched.

check.migrations: Database schema is up to date
check.commandForms: All command forms can be built
check.assistantCatalog: All tools are visible to the assistant
check.tourAnchors: All tour steps point to existing anchors
check.themeRoundtrip: Every theme applies and restores cleanly
check.languageRoundtrip: Language switch works and switches back
check.designDocRoundtrip: Design settings can be written back
check.netUpdateServer: Update server responds with a valid version
check.voteAnonymous: Poll votes are anonymous (hashed device id)
check.modelUrlAllowlist: All model downloads come from huggingface.co
check.namespaceIsolation: Tools cannot read each other's data
check.syncCrypto: Sync encryption seals and verifies correctly
check.syncEngine: Sync engine converges two scratch devices

online.hosts: Contacts only these services: hollatzleif.github.io (website & updates), github.com (update server), cardo-polls.hollatzleif.workers.dev (polls & feed), open-meteo.com (weather), huggingface.co (AI models), googleapis.com (Drive sync). Nothing about you is sent – Cardo only asks whether they respond.

detail.browserSkipped: only available in the desktop app – skipped
detail.noDesignDoc: no design settings saved yet – skipped

market.title: Tool market

privacy.green: Works fully offline – your data never leaves this device.
privacy.yellow: Contacts the internet. Tap for details on what exactly is transmitted.

market.subtitle: All tools ship with Cardo and run locally. Activating just switches them on – the privacy light tells you before anything would ever leave your device.
market.noResults: No tools match your search or filters.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.name: Name
profile.nameRequired: Please tell us your name – it stays on this device.
profile.localNote: These details stay on your device. No account, no cloud.

onboarding.restartHint: You can restart this tour any time in Settings → Help.

welcome.title: Welcome to Cardo!
welcome.body: Cardo is your pivot point: one calm place where you collect and arrange everything – exactly the way you want. This short tour shows you around. You can skip it at any time.

edit.title: Edit mode
edit.body: This button (or Cmd/Ctrl+E) unlocks the canvas: move widgets, resize them, add new ones and create more dashboard pages.

settings.title: Settings & self-test
settings.body: Themes, language, your profile – and the "Run self-test" button that checks the whole app and gives you a report.

market.title: The tool market
market.body: Switch tools on and off here. The privacy light shows for every tool what stays local (green) and what would contact the internet (yellow) – explained in plain language.

palette.title: The command palette
palette.body: Press Cmd/Ctrl+K anywhere to search and run any command – create a task, start the timer, everything is one keystroke away.

privacy.title: Your data stays yours
privacy.body: Cardo works 100% offline. No account, no cloud, no telemetry. Everything lives in one local database and in plain files you own.

templates.title: How would you like to start?
templates.subtitle: Pick a starter layout – everything stays freely rearrangeable. Or start with a blank canvas.

work.title: Work
work.body: Today, tasks, calendar, pomodoro and time tracking.

private.title: Home & life
private.body: Today, routine, habits, countdowns and notes.

focus.title: Deep focus
focus.body: Pomodoro, task board and your productivity stats.

clock.name: Clock
clock.description: A simple clock widget – the first proof that widgets work.

tour.title: This is a widget
tour.body: Every tool lives in a widget like this. In edit mode you can move and resize it.

counter.name: Counter
counter.description: Counts clicks and remembers them – the first proof that storage works.

tour.title: Widgets remember
tour.body: This counter survives restarts. Everything you do in Cardo is stored locally on your device.

alarm.name: Alarm clock
alarm.description: Set alarms that notify you and play a chime – fully local.
alarm.privacy: Works fully offline. Alarm times and messages never leave this device.
alarm.empty: No alarms yet. Add one below.
alarm.messagePlaceholder: What should I remind you of?

msg.notFound: This alarm no longer exists or is disabled.
msg.failed: The alarm could not be triggered.

test.storageRoundtrip: Alarms can be stored and removed
test.nextOccurrence: Next alarm time is computed correctly

tour.title: Your alarms
tour.body: Add an alarm with a time and a message. Cardo notifies you and plays a chime – everything stays on your device.

countdown.name: Countdown
countdown.description: Counts the days until your important events.
countdown.privacy: Works fully offline. Your events never leave this device.
countdown.empty: No countdowns yet. Add an event below.

test.storageRoundtrip: Countdowns can be stored and removed

tour.title: Your countdowns
tour.body: Add an event with a date and Cardo shows you how many days are left – updated daily, fully local.

calendar.name: Calendar
calendar.description: Your own appointments with reminders – fully local, at a glance.
calendar.privacy: Works fully offline. Your appointments never leave this device.

tour.title: Your calendar
tour.body: Click a day to see its appointments and add new ones. Appointments with a time remind you shortly before they start.

notes.name: Notes
notes.description: Markdown notes as real .md files – edit, preview, keep forever.
notes.privacy: Works fully offline. Your notes are plain Markdown (.md) files in a folder you own – readable with any editor, no lock-in.

test.fileRoundtrip: Note files can be written, read and deleted

tour.title: Your notes, your files
tour.body: Every note is a real Markdown file in a folder on your device. Move, back up or open them with any other app – Cardo never locks them in.

msg.noBackend: File access is not available in this context.
msg.notFound: No note with that name exists.
msg.invalidName: That name can't be used for a note.

list.empty: No notes yet. Create your first one.
list.deleteConfirm: Delete note "{{name}}"? The file will be removed from your notes folder.

editor.empty: Select a note or create a new one.

files-explorer.name: Files
files-explorer.description: Browse and edit the files in your Cardo folder – Markdown, text, images, PDF & HTML, Obsidian-style with wiki-links and backlinks.
files-explorer.privacy: Reads and writes files only inside your own Cardo folder. Everything stays on your device; nothing is uploaded.

test.browse: Files can be listed with the right type

files-explorer.invalidName: Please use a name without slashes.
files-explorer.noBackend: File access is not available here.
files-explorer.empty: No files yet. Create one above.
files-explorer.pickHint: Pick a file to view or edit it.
files-explorer.externalHint: This file opens in your system's default app.

pomodoro.name: Pomodoro
pomodoro.description: A fully customizable Pomodoro timer: focused work sessions, short breaks, and a long break after every set of cycles.
pomodoro.privacy: Works fully offline. Your timer state and settings never leave this device.

notify.title: Pomodoro
notify.workDone: Focus session finished – time for a break!
notify.shortBreakDone: Break is over – ready for the next focus session?
notify.longBreakDone: Long break is over – time to get back to it!

settings.sound: Play sound when a phase ends

test.phaseAdvance: Phases advance in the correct order

tour.title: Stay focused
tour.body: Start a focus session and Cardo counts down for you. After each session you earn a break – after a full set of cycles, a long one.
tour.settingsBody: Tap the gear to adjust session and break lengths, how many cycles lead to a long break, and whether a chime plays.

quickcapture.name: Quick capture
quickcapture.description: Capture a thought instantly, sort it later – your GTD inbox.
quickcapture.empty: Inbox empty. Whatever crosses your mind – drop it here and sort it later.

toast.empty: Nothing to capture – the text is empty.

test.itemRoundtrip: Inbox item can be added, read and deleted

tour.title: Your inbox for everything
tour.body: Type a thought and press Enter – it lands here instantly. Later, send it to your To-Do list with one click or delete it. A global shortcut brings this input up from anywhere.

routine.name: Daily routine
routine.description: Your daily checklist – starts fresh every morning, all data stays on this device.

widget.empty: No routine items yet. Add your first one below.

test.itemRoundtrip: Routine items can be stored and deleted
test.dayState: Daily check state is stored per day
test.dateKey: Local date key is computed correctly

tour.title: Your daily routine
tour.body: Check off your routine here every day. At midnight the list starts fresh – and everything stays on this device.

stats.name: Statistics
stats.description: Your productivity at a glance: completed tasks, work time and pomodoros per day, week or month.
stats.empty: Nothing tracked in this range yet. Complete a task, run the work clock or finish a pomodoro – your numbers will show up here.

tour.title: Your numbers
tour.body: See what you've accomplished: tasks, work time and pomodoros – per day, week or month. Everything is counted locally on this device.

todo.name: To-Do
todo.description: Tasks with lists, priorities, categories and due dates – fully local.

list.namePlaceholder: List name… (Enter to create, Esc to cancel)
list.renamePlaceholder: New list name… (Enter to save, Esc to cancel)

todo.empty: Nothing to do here – add a task above.

test.createRoundtrip: Tasks can be created, read back and deleted
test.completeFlow: Completing a task records the completion time
test.queryByList: Tasks can be filtered by list
test.boardStatus: Board status stays in sync with the done flag
test.queryToday: Today query returns correct counts and ordering

intro.title: Your tasks, your lists
intro.body: Type a task and press Enter to add it. The colored dot shows its priority, and overdue due dates turn red.

manage.title: Organize with lists
manage.body: Switch lists with the tabs, create new ones with "+" and rename the active one. Completed tasks collect at the bottom until you clear them.

board.title: Your tasks as a board
board.body: Drag cards between To do, In progress and Done. Dropping a card on Done completes the task – just like ticking its checkbox in the list.

workclock.name: Workclock
workclock.description: Tracks your productive time per day – start, stop, done.
workclock.privacy: Works fully offline. Your times never leave this device.

tour.title: Your workclock
tour.body: Hit start when you begin working and stop when you take a break. Cardo adds up your productive time per day – everything stays on your device.

habits.name: Habits
habits.description: Track your daily habits with streaks and a 26-week heatmap – fully local.

widget.heading: Today
widget.allDone: All habits done – keep the streak alive!
widget.empty: No habits yet. Add your first one below.

test.habitRoundtrip: Habits can be stored and deleted

tour.title: Your habits
tour.body: Check off your habits every day and watch your streaks grow. The heatmap shows your last 26 weeks – and everything stays on this device.

today.name: Today
today.description: Your day at a glance – events, tasks, routine and habits from your other tools.
today.privacy: Works fully offline. Only shows data other Cardo tools on this device expose via commands.

widget.emptyHint: Today shows your day at a glance as soon as To-Do, Calendar, Routine or Habits are active.

tour.title: Your day at a glance
tour.body: Today gathers events, tasks, routine and habits from your other tools – always via the command API, never by touching their data directly.

weather.name: Weather
weather.description: Current weather and a 5-day forecast for your place – with honest data age.

privacy.summary: This tool contacts the free Open-Meteo weather service. Only two things ever leave your device: the city name you type into the search, and the coordinates of the place you picked. Never your name, your files, or anything else about you.
privacy.geo: Only the city name you type into the search field – nothing else.
privacy.forecast: Only the coordinates of your chosen place – never anything about you.

weather.setupHint: Which place's weather do you want? Search for a city to get started.
weather.noResults: No places found. Try a different spelling.
weather.searchError: Couldn't reach the place search right now. Check your connection and try again.
weather.noData: Offline – no weather data yet. Cardo will try again.

msg.noPlace: No place set up yet – open the weather widget and search for your city.
msg.offline: No connection right now – keeping the last weather data.

test.cacheRoundtrip: Weather cache can be stored and removed
test.wmoMapping: Weather codes map to the right icons

tour.title: Weather at a glance
tour.body: Search for your city once and Cardo shows the current weather plus five days ahead. You always see how old the data is – and offline, the last forecast simply stays put.

hydration.name: Hydration
hydration.description: Counts your daily glasses of water and reminds you to drink – fully local.
hydration.privacy: Works fully offline. Your drinking habits never leave this device.

notification.title: Time for a glass of water 💧
notification.body: You're at {{glasses}} of {{goal}} glasses – have a sip!

test.dayRoundtrip: Daily glass count is stored per day
test.windowLogic: Reminder time window is computed correctly

tour.title: Stay hydrated
tour.body: Tap +1 for every glass you drink. Cardo reminds you during your chosen hours until you hit your goal – everything stays on this device.

assistant.name: Assistant
assistant.description: Your local thinking partner: dump your thoughts and turn them into concrete actions with one click – the AI runs entirely on your device.

privacy.summary: The AI runs entirely on your device – your thoughts and data never leave it. Only when you press install does Cardo download a model file from huggingface.co.
privacy.download: Downloads the selected AI model file from huggingface.co – only when you press install. Nothing about you is sent; the request only reveals which model is being fetched.

test.proposalValidate: Proposals are validated, hostile input is filtered
test.profileCrud: Profiles can be created, read and deleted
test.routerParse: Router answers resolve to a team member
test.templateKnown: Every model uses a known prompt template
test.catalogSane: Model catalog entries are complete and valid
test.modelCompetences: Model competences resolve for every model (en/de)

tour.title: Braindump instead of busywork
tour.body: Just write down whatever is on your mind. Your assistant turns it into concrete proposals – you only confirm with Yes, Edit or No. Everything runs locally.

eisenhower.name: Eisenhower Matrix
eisenhower.description: Sort tasks into four urgent/important quadrants and always know what to do next.
eisenhower.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

widget.empty: No tasks yet – add one above and pick its quadrant.

command.addDesc: Adds a task to an Eisenhower quadrant (q1 = urgent & important … q4 = neither).
command.moveDesc: Moves an existing task to another quadrant by its id.
command.completeDesc: Marks a matrix task as done by its id.

test.crud: Tasks survive add, move, complete and delete

tour.title: Eisenhower Matrix
tour.body: Type a task, pick its quadrant and press Enter. The checkbox completes a task, the dropdown moves it – in edit mode you can also delete.

sticky-notes.name: Sticky Notes
sticky-notes.description: A freeform wall of colorful sticky notes – drag them around, recolor and edit inline.
sticky-notes.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

widget.empty: No notes yet – write one above and stick it to the wall.

command.addDesc: Sticks a new note to the wall; the color (chart-1 … chart-8) is optional and rotates otherwise.

test.crud: Notes survive create, read, update and delete
test.zorder: Clicked notes come to the front, the top note stays stable

tour.title: Sticky Notes
tour.body: Write a note and press Enter. Drag notes anywhere, double-click to edit the text and pick a new color – in edit mode you can also delete.

decision-log.name: Decision Log
decision-log.description: A searchable log of your decisions – context, options, decision and rationale as markdown files.
decision-log.privacy: Works fully local. Decisions are saved as markdown files in your folder. Nothing leaves your device.

command.addDesc: Records a decision as a markdown document (title and decision required; context, options and rationale optional).

tour.title: Decision Log
tour.body: Log decisions with context and rationale – each one becomes a searchable markdown file.

reading-list.name: Reading List
reading-list.description: A queue for articles and books – track what you want to read, are reading, and have finished.
reading-list.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

widget.empty: Nothing on your reading list yet.

command.addDesc: Adds an article or book (title, optional http/https link) to the reading list.
command.setStatusDesc: Moves a reading-list item to queued, reading or done (needs the item id).

tour.title: Reading List
tour.body: Collect articles and books, move them from queued to done – and send any item to your notes.

scratchpad.name: Scratchpad
scratchpad.description: One always-there markdown note for quick thoughts – with autosave and live preview.
scratchpad.privacy: Works fully local. The pad is saved as scratchpad.md in your folder. Nothing leaves your device.

widget.clearConfirm: Clear the whole scratchpad? This cannot be undone.

command.appendDesc: Appends a line of text to the scratchpad (great for quickly parking thoughts).
command.clearDesc: Empties the scratchpad completely. Cannot be undone.

test.appendCommand: Append handles newlines and empty pad

tour.title: Scratchpad
tour.body: One always-there note: type and it saves itself – markdown, math and [[wiki-links]] render in the preview.

savings-jar.name: Savings Jar
savings-jar.description: Visual savings goals – watch your jars fill up as you save.
savings-jar.privacy: Works fully offline. Your savings goals never leave this device.

widget.empty: No savings goals yet – add your first one above.

command.addGoalDesc: Creates a new savings goal with a name, target amount and optional target date (yyyy-mm-dd).
command.contribute: Add money to a savings goal
command.contributeDesc: Adds a positive amount to an existing savings goal; goal ids are listed in the savings context.
command.contextDesc: Summarizes all savings goals with their progress, deadlines and ids for the assistant.

msg.invalidDeadline: The target date is not a valid date (yyyy-mm-dd).
msg.notFound: This savings goal no longer exists.

test.crud: Goals can be created, read and deleted
test.contributeCommand: Contributions increase a goal's saved amount

tour.title: Your savings goals
tour.body: Create a goal, add money whenever you put something aside, and watch the jar fill up. Everything stays on this device.

subscriptions.name: Subscriptions
subscriptions.description: Keeps track of recurring costs and shows what they add up to per month.
subscriptions.privacy: Works fully offline. Your subscriptions never leave this device.

widget.empty: No subscriptions yet – add your first one above.
widget.markPaid: Mark {{name}} as paid – rolls the due date forward

command.addDesc: Creates a subscription with name, amount, billing cycle (weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly) and next due date (yyyy-mm-dd).
command.contextDesc: Summarizes all subscriptions, the exact monthly total and the next due dates for the assistant.

msg.invalidDate: The due date is not a valid date (yyyy-mm-dd).

test.crud: Subscriptions can be created, read and deleted

tour.title: Your recurring costs
tour.body: Add your subscriptions once and Cardo shows what they cost per month and what is due next – fully offline.

unit-converter.name: Unit Converter
unit-converter.description: Converts length, weight, temperature, data, speed, volume and area units.
unit-converter.privacy: Works fully offline. Nothing you convert leaves this device.

command.convertDesc: Converts a value between two units (e.g. km→mi, °C→°F, GiB→GB); the category is inferred and data.text carries the formatted answer.
command.contextDesc: Tells the assistant which unit pair was converted last.

test.convertCommand: Convert command answers and remembers the pair

tour.title: Convert anything
tour.body: Pick a category, choose two units and type a value – the result updates live. Decimals and your default category live behind the gear.

world-clock.name: World Clock
world-clock.description: The current time in up to eight places at once – daylight-saving safe.
world-clock.privacy: Works fully offline. Your time zones never leave this device.

widget.empty: No time zones yet – add one above.
widget.invalidZone: Unknown time zone – pick one from the list.

command.addZoneDesc: Adds an IANA time zone (e.g. "America/New_York") to the world clock, with an optional label.
command.contextDesc: Lists the configured time zones with their current local times for the assistant.

msg.zoneExists: This time zone is already on the list.
msg.invalidZone: That is not a valid IANA time zone.
msg.maxZones: The world clock already holds the maximum number of time zones.

test.zonesCrud: Time zones can be added and removed
test.tzValidation: Time zone validation and conversion work

tour.title: The world at a glance
tour.body: Add the places you care about and see their local times side by side – with day and night at a glance.

password-gen.name: Password Generator
password-gen.description: Generates strong passwords and passphrases – fully local, nothing is ever stored.
password-gen.privacy: Works fully offline. Generated passwords are never saved or transmitted – they exist only on your screen.

widget.valueLabel: Generated value – click to select it
widget.noClasses: Enable at least one character set in the settings.

command.generateDesc: Generates a strong random password using the saved preferences; an optional length (8–128) overrides the setting. The password is returned in the result and never stored.

msg.noClasses: No character set enabled – check the generator settings.

test.noPersist: Generating leaves no trace in storage

tour.title: Strong passwords
tour.body: One click gives you a fresh password or passphrase. Nothing is ever saved – copy it straight into the app that needs it.

random-picker.name: Random Picker
random-picker.description: Decision wheel, list picker and dice – let fair randomness decide, fully local.
random-picker.privacy: Works fully offline. Your lists and picks never leave this device.

widget.empty: Add entries in the list view – then the wheel can spin.
widget.removeOnPick: Remove picked entries from the list
widget.invalidDice: Use the NdM format, e.g. 2d6 (max 20 dice, 1000 sides).

command.pickDesc: Picks one entry uniformly at random from the named list (or the first list). Lists marked accordingly consume the picked entry.
command.rollDesc: Rolls dice in NdM notation, e.g. "2d6" for two six-sided dice (max 20 dice with up to 1000 sides). Returns the faces and the total.

msg.empty: Nothing to pick – add a list with entries first.

test.crud: Lists survive create, update and delete
test.pickCommand: Picking returns a member and consumes it
test.diceLogic: Dice parser rejects edge cases, faces stay in range

tour.title: Let chance decide
tour.body: Put your options into a list and spin the wheel – or switch the widget to the list or dice view. Everything stays on this device.

bookmarks.name: Bookmarks
bookmarks.description: Your favorite links as a dashboard – grouped into folders, opened with one click.
bookmarks.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

widget.empty: No bookmarks yet – add a link above.

command.addDesc: Saves an http(s) link with title and optional folder; other URL schemes are rejected.

msg.invalidUrl: Invalid URL – only http:// and https:// links work

test.crud: Links survive create, read and delete

tour.title: Bookmarks
tour.body: Add a link with URL, title and optional folder. A click opens it in your browser – in edit mode you can delete bookmarks.

qr-generator.name: QR Codes
qr-generator.description: Turns text, links and Wi-Fi credentials into QR codes – generated fully offline.
qr-generator.privacy: Works fully offline. Whatever you encode never leaves this device – the QR code is computed locally.

widget.empty: Enter something above – the QR code appears instantly.
widget.tooLong: Too long for a QR code (max. 213 bytes).
widget.batchEmpty: Enter one line per code above.

settings.ecInfo: Error correction level M – about 15% of the code may be damaged and still scan.

command.makeDesc: Encodes the given text as a QR code and shows it in the widget. Fails when the text exceeds the QR capacity (213 bytes).

msg.tooLong: Too long for a QR code (max. 213 bytes).

test.matrix: Encoder produces a structurally valid symbol
test.wifiEscaping: Wi-Fi payload escaping follows the spec

tour.title: QR codes, fully offline
tour.body: Type a text, a link or your Wi-Fi credentials and the QR code appears instantly – computed on this device, ready to save as PNG.

time-blocking.name: Time Blocking
time-blocking.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Time Blocking
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Time Blocking.

project-tracker.name: Projects
project-tracker.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Projects
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Projects.

okr.name: Goals & OKRs
okr.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Goals & OKRs
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Goals & OKRs.

flashcards.name: Flashcards
flashcards.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Flashcards
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Flashcards.

mood.name: Mood Journal
mood.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Mood Journal
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Mood Journal.

sleep-log.name: Sleep Log
sleep-log.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Sleep Log
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Sleep Log.

workout.name: Workout Log
workout.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Workout Log
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Workout Log.

meal-planner.name: Meal Planner
meal-planner.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Meal Planner
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Meal Planner.

medication.name: Medication
medication.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Medication
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Medication.

breathing.name: Breathing
breathing.privacy: Works fully local. Nothing leaves your device.

tour.title: Breathing
tour.body: TODO: one-line tour hint for Breathing.

polls.intro: Vote on where Cardo goes next. Polls are questions from the developer – for example "Should we build feature X?".
polls.privacyNote: Loading polls contacts hollatzleif.github.io (the questions) and a Cloudflare server (the vote counts). When you vote, only the poll ID, your choice and an anonymous device hash are sent – one vote per installation, no name, no profile. Nothing is loaded until you press the button.
polls.none: No polls right now. Check back later!
polls.error: The poll server is unreachable right now. Cardo works normally – try again later.

focus.title: Focus mode
focus.taskPlaceholder: What are you working on right now?
focus.pomodoroMissing: The Pomodoro tool is not active. Activate it in the tool market to see your timer here – focus mode works without it, too.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.optinIntro: News from Cardo: announcements and polls from the developer land here – for example "Should we build feature X?".
inbox.privacyNote: If you enable this, Cardo checks cardo-polls.hollatzleif.workers.dev for news on launch and once per hour. Nothing about you is sent. Voting transmits only the poll ID, your choice and an anonymous device hash. You can switch this off here at any time.
inbox.empty: No news right now. Check back later!

widget.noModel: No AI model installed yet. Set up your assistant in Settings → Assistant.
widget.parseError: The model's answer was unusable. Just try again.
widget.generateError: Something went wrong. Check in Settings → Assistant whether your model can be loaded.
widget.noProfiles: No assistant set up yet. Create one in Settings → Assistant.
widget.ramError: Not enough memory to load this model. Close other apps or pick a smaller model.
widget.blocked: Skipped – this command is outside this assistant's allowed tools.

qwen3-0-6b.strengths: Tiny and loads in a flash; runs on virtually any machine.
qwen3-0-6b.weaknesses: Very limited understanding – anything beyond simple requests confuses it.
qwen3-0-6b.idealFor: Trying the assistant out, or as a quick router model.

qwen3-1-7b.strengths: Solid small all-rounder with decent German and reliable structured output.
qwen3-1-7b.weaknesses: Shallow reasoning; misses details in longer braindumps.
qwen3-1-7b.idealFor: Fast everyday commands on 8 GB machines.

qwen3-4b.strengths: Good all-rounder: solid German and dependable JSON output for its size.
qwen3-4b.weaknesses: Noticeably slower on 8 GB machines; complex requests can overwhelm it.
qwen3-4b.idealFor: The sensible default on most machines.

qwen3-8b.strengths: Strong all-rounder with good German and very reliable structured output.
qwen3-8b.weaknesses: Needs 16 GB RAM to feel smooth.
qwen3-8b.idealFor: Main assistant on a 16 GB Apple Silicon Mac.

qwen3-14b.strengths: Very capable all-rounder; handles long, messy braindumps well.
qwen3-14b.weaknesses: Big download, needs plenty of RAM and replies noticeably slower.
qwen3-14b.idealFor: Quality-first main profile on machines with 16 GB or more.

qwen3-30b-a3b.strengths: Mixture-of-experts: near-14B quality at the speed of a ~3B model.
qwen3-30b-a3b.weaknesses: Huge download, and the full weights must fit in RAM (~24 GB).
qwen3-30b-a3b.idealFor: The best quality/speed mix on 32 GB Apple Silicon.

phi-4-mini.strengths: Remarkably clever for its size, especially at logic and structure.
phi-4-mini.weaknesses: Leans towards English – German replies can sound stiff.
phi-4-mini.idealFor: Fast English-language tasks on small machines.

phi-4.strengths: Very smart in the 14B class; strong reasoning and clean output.
phi-4.weaknesses: Leans towards English; German is usable but not its strength.
phi-4.idealFor: Complex reasoning tasks, preferably in English.

mistral-7b.weaknesses: Older generation – weaker German and less precise than newer 7–8B models.
mistral-7b.idealFor: A dependable everyday model on mid-range hardware.

mistral-small-24b.strengths: Very strong across the board, close to much larger models.
mistral-small-24b.weaknesses: Needs about 20 GB RAM and is not fast.

granite-3-1-8b.strengths: Built for function calling – very reliable command and JSON output.
granite-3-1-8b.weaknesses: Plain, sober writing; not the most creative.
granite-3-1-8b.idealFor: Profiles that mostly execute commands with structured output.

deepseek-r1-7b.strengths: Strong step-by-step reasoning for tricky, logical problems.
deepseek-r1-7b.weaknesses: Thinks out loud before answering, which makes it slow (the visible reasoning is stripped automatically).
deepseek-r1-7b.idealFor: Puzzling tasks where quality matters more than speed.

llama-3-2-1b.strengths: Tiny and instant; surprisingly capable for its size.
llama-3-2-1b.weaknesses: Very limited depth; Meta license notice applies.
llama-3-2-1b.idealFor: Router duty and very simple commands on any machine.

llama-3-2-3b.strengths: Strong for its size and pleasantly quick.
llama-3-2-3b.weaknesses: Limited depth on complex requests; Meta license notice applies.
llama-3-2-3b.idealFor: A snappy assistant on 8 GB machines.

llama-3-1-8b.strengths: Strong all-rounder with broad knowledge and good multilingual skills.
llama-3-1-8b.weaknesses: Meta license notice applies; needs ~8 GB RAM.

gemma-3-4b.strengths: Outstanding German and writing quality for its size.
gemma-3-4b.weaknesses: You must accept Google's Gemma terms before downloading.

gemma-3-12b.strengths: Excellent German, natural style, very good multilingual writing.
gemma-3-12b.weaknesses: Needs ~11 GB RAM; Google's Gemma terms must be accepted.
gemma-3-12b.idealFor: A German-first main assistant on 16 GB machines.

gemma-3-27b.strengths: The best German writing quality in this catalog.
gemma-3-27b.weaknesses: Very high RAM needs (~22 GB), slow; Gemma terms must be accepted.
gemma-3-27b.idealFor: Maximum text quality on 32 GB machines.

claude-fable-5.strengths: The most capable Claude model – strongest on complex, multi-step and agentic work.
claude-fable-5.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-fable-5.idealFor: The hardest tasks, where quality matters more than speed.

claude-opus-4-8.strengths: Very strong all-round model with high quality across almost every kind of task.
claude-opus-4-8.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-opus-4-8.idealFor: Demanding everyday work that needs consistently high quality.

claude-sonnet-5.strengths: Fast and strong at the same time – handles everyday tasks quickly and reliably.
claude-sonnet-5.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-sonnet-5.idealFor: The brisk default for day-to-day work.

claude-haiku-4-5.strengths: The fastest Claude model – answers almost instantly.
claude-haiku-4-5.weaknesses: Needs internet and an active Claude subscription. Your chat content and the workspace files Claude reads are sent to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota – not local, not private.
claude-haiku-4-5.idealFor: Light tasks, short questions and quick commands.

wizard.checkIntro: Your device: {{ram}} RAM, {{cores}} cores, {{chip}}. Pick a model – the recommended one fits your hardware best.
wizard.loadSmoke: Loading the model for the first time…
wizard.downloadError: Download or loading failed. Check your internet connection and try again.
wizard.doneBody: Your assistant is ready. Add the Assistant widget to your dashboard and dump your thoughts.

persona.nameQuestion: What should your assistant be called?

manage.deleteConfirm: Really delete this model? The file is removed from your device; you can reinstall it any time.
manage.switchKeepHint: Your assistant stays the same – only the model behind it changes.
manage.switchFreshHint: Personality, working style and memory are reset to their post-setup state.
manage.askBeforeExecuteHint: Recommended: every proposal waits for your Yes. If you turn this off, the assistant executes valid proposals immediately.
manage.advancedHint: These three files drive your assistant. Cardo normally manages them automatically – only change them if you know what you're doing.
manage.delegationAskHint: If another assistant is better suited for a task, Cardo suggests handing it over.
manage.deleteBlockedByProfile: Used by {{names}} – assign a different model to those assistants first.

team.empty: This team has no usable members. Check the team setup in Settings → Assistant.

profiles.sectionIntro: Create several assistants with their own strengths – e.g. Hanna for work, Felix for letters – sharing one memory so nothing gets lost.
profiles.importError: Import failed – the file could not be read.
profiles.deleteConfirm: Really delete "{{name}}"? Its memory is kept and can be reused.

teams.title: Teams
teams.deleteConfirm: Really delete team "{{name}}"? The assistants are kept.
teams.needTwo: You need at least two assistants for a team.

flow.templateTitle: What should your new assistant start as?
flow.willDownload: Will be downloaded when you create it
flow.competencesLabel: What is this assistant strong at?
flow.competencesHint: One line per strength – Cardo uses this for delegation suggestions.
flow.modelDrivenHint: Your assistant's strengths are determined by the model you choose.

secretary.name: Secretary
secretary.desc: Writes letters & emails and keeps track of tasks.
secretary.extra: You mainly help with writing letters, emails and notes, and you keep an eye on open tasks.
secretary.competences:
Writing letters and emails
Managing tasks and reminders

planner.name: Planner
planner.desc: Structures appointments, routines and your day.
planner.extra: You mainly help with appointments, routines and day planning, and you think ahead about preparations.
planner.competences:
Appointments and day planning
Routines and habits

coach.name: Coach
coach.desc: Motivates you with habits, breaks and goals.
coach.extra: You gently motivate with habits, breaks and personal goals, without applying pressure.
coach.competences:
Habits and motivation
Breaks and balance

blank.name: Blank
blank.desc: Start from scratch – you answer all questions yourself.

memory.whyShare: A shared memory is usually the better choice: all assistants know the same facts, names and preferences, and nothing gets lost when you switch between them. A separate memory only makes sense when one assistant must keep things strictly apart.

tools.pickHint: The assistant may only use these tools:

license.consentText: "{{model}}" is provided under the {{name}} terms. Please read and confirm them before the download starts.

competence.banner: "{{name}}" successfully proposed {{count}} {{tool}} tasks – add this as a strength?

advanced.hint: These files drive your assistants. Cardo normally manages them automatically – only change them if you know what you're doing.
advanced.memoryDeleteBlocked: A memory can only be deleted once no assistant or team uses it any more.
advanced.memoryDeleteConfirm: Permanently delete memory "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
advanced.teamCompetencesHint: Automatically maintained overview of who is best at what – the basis for delegation suggestions. Your '## Notizen' section is preserved.

chat.contextHint: This chat has gotten quite long – that can slow the model down and confuse it. Type /clearchat to start fresh.

claude.guideTitle: Set up Claude in three steps
claude.guideStep1: Install Claude Code from claude.com/claude-code (macOS: curl installer or Homebrew).
claude.guideStep2: Open a terminal, run `claude` once and sign in with your Anthropic account (Pro or Max subscription).
claude.guideStep3: Come back here and press “Check again”.
claude.cloudMeta: Cloud model · runs via your Anthropic account · nothing to download
claude.notReady: Not set up – Claude Code CLI missing
claude.consentText: Claude does not run on your device: it needs an internet connection and an active Claude subscription (Pro/Max). Your chat content AND the workspace files Claude reads are transmitted to Anthropic, and every request uses your plan's quota. The local models remain the private choice – with them, nothing leaves your device.
claude.consentCheckbox: I understand that content is transmitted to Anthropic and that my subscription quota is used.
claude.notInstalled: Claude Code was not found on this machine. Set it up under Settings → Assistant (Claude section), then try again.
claude.authError: Claude could not sign in. Open a terminal, run `claude` once and sign in with your Anthropic account – then it will work here again.
claude.timeout: Claude took too long and the request was cancelled. Try again – for long conversations /clearchat helps.
claude.generateError: The request to Claude failed. Check your internet connection and the Claude status under Settings → Assistant.
claude.teamNeedsLocalLeader: Teams need a local model for routing – Claude assistants can be members but not team leaders. Add at least one member with a local model.

msg.created: File saved to your notes folder.
msg.failed: That didn't work – check the file name (.md/.txt/.csv/.json).

command.nowDesc: Runs one sync round with the configured transport.
command.statusDesc: Reports whether sync is set up, pending changes and connected devices.

msg.desktopOnly: Sync only runs in the desktop app.

context.disabled: Sync: key present, but turned off on this device.
context.active: Sync: on via {{transport}} · {{pending}} changes waiting · {{devices}} device(s) · last round: {{last}}.

command.exportDesc: Saves all dashboard pages (and optionally the design) as a shareable JSON file.
command.importDesc: Appends the pages of an exported board file. Params: json (inline board JSON, optional), applyDesign (boolean, optional).

msg.importedPartial: Board imported – some tools in the file are unknown and were skipped.
msg.invalid: That is not a Cardo board file.
msg.desktopOnly: Import from file works in the desktop app.
